2 — India's position in global bentonite trade
India is the world's largest exporter of bentonite — supplying to over 80 countries across Southeast Asia, the Middle East, Africa, Europe and the Americas. India accounts for 50% of global
, ahead of China at 18% and Turkey at 8%.
The reason is geology. The Kutch region of Gujarat — specifically the
district — hosts one of the world's finest natural sodium bentonite deposits. The

produce high-quality sodium montmorillonite with exceptional swelling, viscosity and thixotropic properties. Rajasthan contributes calcium bentonite, widely used for industrial, agricultural and cosmetic applications.
1.56 million MT of bentonite in 2023 valued at $90.8 million — to Indonesia, Malaysia, China, Romania, South Africa and 75+ other countries.
— Sodium vs calcium bentonite — which do you need?
The single most important distinction in bentonite i
s the type of exchangeable cation — sodium or calcium. This determines swelling behaviour and therefore which applications it suits.
: India produces both natural sodium bentonite (premium, from Kutch) and soda ash activated bentonite (calcium bentonite treated with
₃ to improve swelling). The activated type is cost-effective for applications where extreme swelling is not m
andatory. Always specify which type your application requires when enquiring.
API grade drilling bentonite acts as a
and filtration control agent for water-based mud systems — controls borehole stability, extracts drill cuttings and cools the drill bit
Foundry grade binds sand grains into desired mould shapes, retains me
chanical form and makes mould surface impermeable — high green strength and thermal stability essential
grades available on request for specialty applications
Used in drilling muds to control borehole stability, increase viscosity, reduce fluid loss and form thin filter cake — 1 tonne of bentonite prepares approximately 100 barrels of mud
API 13A Section 9 for oil well drilling; OCMA (Section 11) for geotechnical and water well drilling
Export buyers: Gulf drilling contractors (UAE, Saudi, Qatar, Oman), Southeast Asian oil companies, African drilling operations
ost all iron, steel and malleable iron casting foundries use bentonite — binds sand grains into mould shapes, makes surface impermeable and retains mechanical form
Kutch sodium bentonite valued for high green strength, dry strength and thermal stability in
Buyers: foundries in Indonesia, Malaysia, Vietnam, Bangladesh, Turkey, Egypt
Thixotropic support agent in diaphragm walls, foundations, tunnelling, horizontal directional drilling (HDD) and pipe jackin
Large infrastructure projects — metro tunnels, highway construction, river crossings — consume bentonite slurry in large volumes
Strong demand in Southeast Asia and Middle East where infrastructure development is most active
in iron ore pellet production — mixed with iron ore fines to produce green pellets before firing
Large volume application in China, Brazil and India's own steel sector
Very low permeability to water — employed to make
porous medium water-tight in landfill caps, pond liners and containment structures
Clay Liners (GCL) use sodium bentonite sandwiched between geotextiles
is a slow-release sulphur fertiliser for sulphur-deficient soils
Large export market in Southeast Asia, Australia and Latin America
Other applications: cat litter (Middle East, Asia) · animal feed binder · paper manufacturing pitch control · cosmetics and
skin care · pharmaceuticals · dyes and paints
Geology advantage: Kutch sodium bentonite is naturally high in montmorillonite — minimal activation needed for premium grades, unlike many origins requiring heavy soda ash treatment
below US sodium bentonite and competitive with Turkish origin for most Southeast Asian and African buyers
Freight advantage: for Malaysia, Vietnam, Indonesia, Bangladesh, Sri Lanka and Gulf buyers —
ports offer shorter transit and lo
wer freight than US or European origins
Full processing range: from raw lumps to premium API-certified powder — complete gra
Fixed Kutch / Rajasthan source — consistent montmorillonite lot to lot
Full COA per lot: montmorillonite %, swelling index, moisture, viscosity, screen residue
API 13A Section 9 & 11 compliance certificate per consignment
SGS / Intertek pre-shipment inspection available on request
MSDS, COO, packing list with every shipment
All grades from one supplier — API, OCMA, foundry, civil, calcium
MT — easy trial before full container commitment
Competitive FOB / CIF pricing to your port
Section 8 — Mine owners & processors — Welcome to
We source bentonite from Gujarat and Rajasthan — if you are a mine owner, processor or grinding unit with consistent quality material and regular production capacity, we want to hear from you.
ow 14%, proper screening and grinding — and the ability to supply reliably month after month.
Send us: Company · Location (district) · Grade produced · Monthly capacity (MT) · Typical swelling index · Contact number
sing chain: open quarry → jaw crushing → rotary drying → Raymond milling / ball milling → soda ash activation (where required) → air classification and screening → packaging
Kutch district, Gujarat — world-class natural sodium bentonite;
area; primary source for API and OCMA drilling grade
/ Bikaner, Rajasthan — calcium bentonite; primary source for industrial, agricultural and cosmetic grade
area, Gujarat — mixed deposits; foundry and civil grade
Packaging: 50kg HDPE bags · 25kg bags · 500kg / 1MT jumbo bags · bulk FCL for large foundry and
One 20-ft FCL: approximately 22–24 MT of bagged bentonite powder
Transit: Gulf 8–12 days · SE Asia 12–18 days · East Africa 14–18 days · Romania / Europe 20–
Incoterms: FOB Mundra / Kandla · CIF · CFR
Lead time: ex-stock 3–7 days · fresh production run 12–18 days

→ high swelling, used in drilling & foundry
→ low swelling, used as filler & absorbent
